behaviors.description: Trim conversation history to most recent 10 messages before sending to the LLM when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam (history manager -> prompt builder -> LLM client payload). MOCK-MISS: failure is often at handoff (wrong slice/order/count in outgoing request), not just inside one helper function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; a stub LLM client can assert sent messages. LLM-DEP: not dependent on model judgment. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about 'passed to the LLM' payload shape, which can be validated in-process without real HTTP.
behaviors.test_description: Wire chat service + prompt assembly + LLM client adapter with a capturing stub client; feed >10-message history and assert outbound request contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: Generated response should address the customer’s question (not generic/off-topic).
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: model-judgment seam. MOCK-MISS: stubbed outputs will always look 'correct' and hide relevance failures. REAL-INFRA: real model/API call is needed, but the decisive seam is AI output quality. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness varies with real model behavior. MULTI-STEP: single generation quality check, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts against the real production-like model with a labeled set of customer questions; score whether answers are on-topic and question-responsive, and fail below threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects 'too formal' for casual-tier customers and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: model-judgment seam (tone classification) plus module seam (trigger). MOCK-MISS: mocked tone checker can hide real misclassification of formality. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess dependency beyond normal service call path. LLM-DEP: yes, 'correctly identifies' tone requires real model judgment. MULTI-STEP: regeneration trigger is orchestration, but minimum real seam can be exercised with one real tone-judgment step and asserted trigger side effect. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text focuses on identification + trigger, not validating quality across multiple real agentic outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Provide candidate responses for casual-tier scenarios to the real tone-check component, assert formal drafts are flagged, and verify regeneration call is invoked exactly when flagged.

behaviors.description: Persist full exchange (user input, initial generation, any regenerations) with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam to persistence layer (service -> repository -> DB). MOCK-MISS: pure unit tests can miss transaction/serialization mistakes across modules. REAL-INFRA: local DB integration is sufficient; no real network/OS primitive required. LLM-DEP: not dependent on model quality. MULTI-STEP: sequence exists, but not multi-agentic correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute chat flow with one forced regeneration against a real local test DB; assert all exchange records exist, linked correctly, and timestamps are stored in expected fields/order.

behaviors.description: If tone-check regeneration occurs, only the second response is shown to the customer; rejected draft is never exposed.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam in orchestration (generation -> tone gate -> response presenter). MOCK-MISS: unit tests of individual functions can miss leakage at handoff to presenter/API layer. REAL-INFRA: can be caught in-process without real network/subprocess. LLM-DEP: correctness is routing/selection logic, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: orchestration is multi-step, but agentic realism is not required to reproduce leak bug. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text is about which response is exposed, which can be validated with deterministic stub generations.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end service flow with deterministic first/second model stubs and forced tone-fail on first; assert delivered customer payload equals second response and first appears only in internal logs.
